semantic.role: Infrastructure utility that normalizes boolean and positive-integer environment variable parsing for pre-commit configuration.
responsibilities: Provide a canonical environment variable name for pre-commit mode: SPINE_PRECOMMIT.
Parse string environment values into boolean (true/false/undefined) with case-insensitive matching of common truthy/falsy strings.
Parse string environment values into positive integers (or undefined) with strict validation that the parsed number is finite and positive.
outOfScope: Interaction with file system, configuration files, or other config sources.
Error handling for non-string types (caller responsibility).
Reading or setting environment variables; only string-to-value conversion.
invariants: parseBooleanEnv returns undefined only when input is undefined; otherwise always returns true or false.
parsePositiveIntegerEnv returns undefined if input is undefined, not a parseable integer, not finite, or ≤ 0.
Both functions are pure and side-effect free.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide reusable, consistent environment variable parsing for pre-commit detection and configuration across the ArchSpine codebase.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: Part of the v1.0.0 launch of ArchSpine's architectural governance and semantic layer, establishing the pre-commit infrastructure needed for automated policy checks.

publicSurface.description: The environment variable name ('SPINE_PRECOMMIT') that signals the system is running inside a pre-commit hook. Consumed by pre-commit lifecycle scripts and the CLI's pre-commit detection logic.

Converts a string environment value into a boolean by matching common truthy ('1','true','on','yes') and falsy ('0','false','off','no') strings (case-insensitive). Returns undefined on undefined input. Used to read SPINE_PRECOMMIT and similar flags.

Parses a string environment value into a positive integer after validating that the result is finite and greater than zero. Returns undefined on undefined, non-parseable, non-positive, or non-finite input. Used for numeric configuration values like timeouts or limits.
